rows.notes: Page title / header clearly centered at top. Very legible cursive heading.
Pounds '156' clearly aligned in pounds column; shillings '4' and pence '0' are unambiguous. Description 'S. Newtn' is slightly abbreviated but alignment and letters visible.
Amount reads '30.0.0' very clearly. Description 'Gwinear' is legible as shown; fractional year '1/2' visible.

rows.description: Kiddington Rectory Money Rent for 3 years
rows.notes: Amount '60.0.0' clear. The place name reads 'Kiddington' (handwritten double-k/diagonal strokes) and text 'Money Rent for 3 years' visible though slightly cramped.
Amount columns well aligned: '17.15.7'. Description 'Kiddington Mills' and 'Corn Rent' readable; minor ink flourish on 'Mills' but digits clear.

rows.description: Long With Rectory 1 year Corn Rent
rows.notes: Pounds '108' and shillings '3' clear. Pence appears as '9' with a trailing '3q' style loop indicating three‑quarters (¾) — interpreted as 0.75. Description 'Long With' uncertain on exact spelling but context (Rectory, Corn Rent) is clear.
Amount reads '8.17.9' with the same 3/4 trailing mark on pence as previous line. 'Garsington Sadler' reading is plausible from cursive; some ink flourish on 'Sadler' but amount alignment is solid.

Amounts again read as '8.17.9¾' (pence with 3/4 mark). Place name appears similar to previous (Garsington) but second word uncertain (read as 'Prickett'); cursive letters ambiguous. Alignment of numbers supports the reading.

Description partly obscured by a blot/overwriting; I read the place as 'Tintinhull' and a note 'half a year'. Amount repeats the '8.17.9¾' pattern but the description is ambiguous—hence lower confidence.
Amount clearly '4.5.11' with a half‑penny symbol (loop/ob) after the pence — interpreted as 0.5. The second word of the place is uncertain (read as 'Briaton' or 'Braston'); overall readable.
Amount '1.5.2' clear. Name 'Alexander Smith' is legible though slightly cramped; 'Montacute' reading fits the letter shapes.
Amount '0.10.0' (10 shillings) clearly written. 'Clifton Ferry' description legible.
Amount '8.0.8' clear. Description reads as 'S. Newt Tenement' (abbreviated 'S. Newt'); letters are readable and align with common entries above.
Amount '2.13.0' clear. 'Merton Rectory' and 'Corn Rent' are legible.
Amount '9.0.0' unambiguous. 'Chase Hill' clearly read.

Pounds and shillings ('12.3') clear. Pence looks like '10' with a small quarter‑mark (a tiny 'q' style) — interpreted as 0.25. The quarter mark is faint; description spelling 'Benson' is probable but handwriting slightly ambiguous.
Amount reads '6.19.5' clearly. Place name is indistinct ('Tretlewyn' is a best reading from the cursive), 'half a year' is visible. Moderate uncertainty on description spelling.
Amount '3.0.0' clear. 'Monkhirt' (or similar) is partially faded; context 'Rectory 1 year' readable. Chose a conservative confidence.
Amount '20.0.0' clear. Description reads as 'Yardington Rectory & Tennement 3 years' though second word spelling is approximated from handwriting.
Amount looks like '56.3.4' with a small half‑penny loop after the pence — read as 4½ (0.5). 'Thrupp' (or similar) and '3 years and a half' are legible in context.

Amount '8.0.0' clear. 'La Lane' and '3 Tenaments 2 years' present though final spelling of place ambiguous; numbers are definitive.
Amount '34.6.8' legible. Place reads as 'Meddenham' (could be 'Medenham') but letters are reasonably clear.
Amount '13.6.8' clear. Description likely refers to 'Rump St Martin' or 'Remp St Martin'—the initial letter uncertain but context indicates a parish name; digits are unambiguous.

rows.description: Padbury St Mary Magdalen Parish 8 years
rows.notes: Amount reads '3.16.0' clearly. Description is long; interpreted as 'Padbury St Mary Magdalen Parish 8 years' though some letters are cramped and spacing ambiguous; nevertheless the place and parish phrase fit the handwriting.

rows.description: Sir G. O. Turnor for Levett Close at Westcot 4 yrs
rows.notes: Amount '11.6.8' clear. The personal name/title is abbreviated and the location 'Levett Close' / 'Westcot' are best readings from cursive; several letters uncertain so confidence reduced.
Amount '6.10.10' visible though pence '10' digits are slightly crowded. 'Hertford Cole' reading plausible; handwriting for name reasonably clear.
Amount '3.6.8' readable. Given proximity to previous 'Cole' entry, this reads as 'Jenes Cole' (or 'Jenes/Jeus Cole'); name letters are slightly unclear but amount is unambiguous.
Amount '0.10.0' (10 shillings) clear. 'Back Gate Coffin' reading fits the cursive; letters are legible.

rows.description: Little Tew late Mrs Heywood now Kimmerfrom
rows.notes: Amount '1.1.11' readable but small and close to margin; description is long and several names ('Heywood', 'Kimmerfrom'/'Kimmerfrom?') are uncertain — chosen best‑fit reading from cursive. Lower confidence due to crowded text and uncommon name spellings.

The description 'Salford 1/2 a year' is visible at the bottom left but there is little or no corresponding amount visible on the right margin (possibly cut off or not entered). Serious ambiguity about amount presence; left blank for amounts.
